turns.svg:
<svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" width="512" height="512" viewBox="0 0 512 512">
  <defs>
    <radialGradient id="bg" cx="50%" cy="35%" r="70%">
      <stop offset="0%" stop-color="#fff6fb"/>
      <stop offset="60%" stop-color="#f3fbff"/>
      <stop offset="100%" stop-color="#f7f7ff"/>
    </radialGradient>
    <linearGradient id="petal" x1="0" y1="0" x2="0" y2="1">
      <stop offset="0%" stop-color="#ff6aa2"/>
      <stop offset="100%" stop-color="#ff3f7f"/>
    </linearGradient>
    <linearGradient id="heart" x1="0" y1="0" x2="1" y2="1">
      <stop offset="0%" stop-color="#ff3b6f"/>
      <stop offset="100%" stop-color="#ff8bb0"/>
    </linearGradient>
    <filter id="softShadow" x="-30%" y="-30%" width="160%" height="160%">
      <feDropShadow dx="0" dy="8" stdDeviation="10" flood-color="#7a2a4a" flood-opacity="0.22"/>
    </filter>
  </defs>

  <rect width="512" height="512" fill="url(#bg)"/>

  <!-- Sparkles -->
  <g fill="#ffd34d" opacity="0.95">
    <path d="M86 120l10 7-12 3 7 11-11-6-6 12-3-13-12-2 11-7-8-11 13 4z"/>
    <path d="M155 86l7 5-8 2 5 7-8-4-4 8-2-9-8-1 8-5-5-7 9 3z" fill="#ffe27a"/>
    <path d="M426 150l10 7-12 3 7 11-11-6-6 12-3-13-12-2 11-7-8-11 13 4z"/>
    <path d="M390 95l8 6-9 2 6 8-9-5-4 10-2-11-10-2 9-6-6-8 11 3z" fill="#ffe27a"/>
    <path d="M110 270l9 6-10 2 6 9-10-5-5 10-2-11-10-2 9-6-6-9 11 3z"/>
  </g>

  <!-- Flower petals (around heart) -->
  <g filter="url(#softShadow)" transform="translate(0,0)">
    <g transform="translate(256 290)">
      <!-- petals -->
      <g>
        <path d="M0 -210 C60 -210 70 -150 20 -130 C45 -160 40 -200 0 -210 Z" fill="url(#petal)" opacity="0.98"/>
        <path d="M0 -210 C-60 -210 -70 -150 -20 -130 C-45 -160 -40 -200 0 -210 Z" fill="url(#petal)" opacity="0.98"/>

        <path d="M0 -150 C70 -150 110 -95 70 -70 C95 -90 80 -125 0 -150 Z" fill="url(#petal)" opacity="0.92"/>
        <path d="M0 -150 C-70 -150 -110 -95 -70 -70 C-95 -90 -80 -125 0 -150 Z" fill="url(#petal)" opacity="0.92"/>

        <path d="M0 -120 C80 -105 125 -35 75 -15 C105 -30 90 -75 0 -120 Z" fill="url(#petal)" opacity="0.9"/>
        <path d="M0 -120 C-80 -105 -125 -35 -75 -15 C-105 -30 -90 -75 0 -120 Z" fill="url(#petal)" opacity="0.9"/>

        <path d="M0 -75 C90 -50 95 20 40 25 C65 5 50 -35 0 -75 Z" fill="url(#petal)" opacity="0.88"/>
        <path d="M0 -75 C-90 -50 -95 20 -40 25 C-65 5 -50 -35 0 -75 Z" fill="url(#petal)" opacity="0.88"/>
      </g>

      <!-- heart center -->
      <path d="M0 85
               C-60 35 -120 -5 -90 -55
               C-65 -95 -15 -85 0 -55
               C15 -85 65 -95 90 -55
               C120 -5 60 35 0 85 Z" 
            fill="url(#heart)" stroke="#ff1f5f" stroke-opacity="0.25" stroke-width="4"/>

      <!-- inner glow -->
      <path d="M0 70
               C-45 30 -95 0 -73 -38
               C-55 -68 -18 -60 -3 -35
               C-2 -38 2 -38 3 -35
               C18 -60 55 -68 73 -38
               C95 0 45 30 0 70 Z" fill="#ffd1e1" opacity="0.55"/>

      <!-- center highlight -->
      <path d="M-10 40 C-35 15 -60 -5 -45 -28 C-30 -50 -5 -48 -2 -28 C-1 -45 12 -52 26 -46 C48 -36 35 -5 10 20 Z" fill="#fff" opacity="0.35"/>
    </g>
  </g>

  <!-- Subtle ground/foil for composition -->
  <ellipse cx="256" cy="460" rx="120" ry="22" fill="#000" opacity="0.06"/>
</svg>

messages.content:
You are a drawing agent in a one-word guessing game.
Return JSON only, with a single field: svg.

Rules:
1) Draw the target word using only SVG graphics.
2) Output one complete <svg>...</svg> string.
3) Do not include text labels, letters, or numbers in the drawing.
4) Keep the SVG simple and valid. No scripts, no foreignObject.
5) Use a 512x512 canvas.

Output schema:
{ "svg": "string" }

message.reasoning:
**Creating a visual representation**

I'm thinking of how to represent the word "beautiful" using only shapes, without any text. I’ll go with common symbols like a heart and flowers. A heart with sparkles and flower elements sounds perfect! I’ll aim for a simple SVG design on a 512 canvas. The background will be light, with flower petals around the heart center and sparkles scattered. Once it's ready, I’ll provide it in JSON format. Let's get crafting!

messages.content:
You are the guesser in a visual one-word guessing game.
Return JSON only, with a single field: guesses.

Rules:
1) You receive one JPEG image of a drawing.
2) Return exactly 20 one-word guesses in order of confidence (best guess first).
3) The order is important and will be scored by first correct position.
4) Do not repeat guesses.
5) Each guess must be EN-US one word, no spaces.
6) Avoid punctuation and numbers.

Output schema:
{ "guesses": ["string", "... exactly 20 items ..."] }

It could represent themes like "love," "valentine," or even “romance.” I want to focus on words that capture affection and care. I’ll brainstorm around 20 unique options, making sure they’re all one word without spaces or punctuation. This should be fun and creative, as I explore different ways to represent those warm feelings!
